Handles incoming and removed phone call events and manages missed call notifications for ANCS.

# Purpose
The code manages phone call events using the Apple Notification Center Service (ANCS) on a Pebble device. It includes functions to handle incoming and removed phone call notifications, as well as to manage missed call events. The function [`ancs_phone_call_handle_incoming`](<#ancs_phone_call_handle_incoming>) processes incoming call notifications by extracting caller information and dispatching a phone event. It uses the [`prv_strip_formatting_chars`](<#prv_strip_formatting_chars>) function to clean up the caller's name or number by removing formatting characters. The function [`ancs_phone_call_handle_removed`](<#ancs_phone_call_handle_removed>) handles the removal of call notifications by dispatching a hide event.

The code also includes functionality to temporarily block missed call notifications. The function [`ancs_phone_call_temporarily_block_missed_calls`](<#ancs_phone_call_temporarily_block_missed_calls>) sets a timer to block missed call notifications for a specified duration. The timer is managed using the `RegularTimerInfo` structure and related functions, such as `regular_timer_add_multisecond_callback` and `regular_timer_remove_callback`. The function [`ancs_phone_call_should_ignore_missed_calls`](<#ancs_phone_call_should_ignore_missed_calls>) checks if the missed call blocking timer is active. The code integrates with other components through included headers, such as `ancs_phone_call.h`, `applib/graphics/utf8.h`, and `services/normal/phone_call_util.h`, to provide its functionality.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `ancs_phone_call.h`
- `applib/graphics/utf8.h`
- `kernel/events.h`
- `services/common/regular_timer.h`
- `services/normal/phone_call_util.h`
- `stdbool.h`
- `stdint.h`
- `string.h`


# Global Variables

---
### s\_missed\_call\_timer\_id
- **Type**: ``RegularTimerInfo``
- **Description**: Holds information about a timer used to manage missed call notifications. The timer is used to temporarily block missed call notifications for a specified duration.
- **Use**: Used to schedule and manage a timer that blocks missed call notifications for a short period.


# Functions

---
### prv\_put\_call\_event<!-- {{#callable:prv_put_call_event}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/ancs/ancs_phone_call.c#L30>)

Creates and sends a `PebbleEvent` for a phone call event with specified parameters.
- **Inputs**:
    - `type`: The type of phone event, represented by `PhoneEventType`.
    - `call_identifier`: A unique identifier for the phone call, represented as a `uint32_t`.
    - `caller`: A pointer to a `PebblePhoneCaller` structure containing caller information.
    - `ios_9`: A boolean indicating if the source is iOS 9 or not.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initializes a `PebbleEvent` structure with the event type set to `PEBBLE_PHONE_EVENT`.
    - Sets the `phone` field of the `PebbleEvent` with the provided `type`, `call_identifier`, and `caller`.
    - Determines the `source` field based on the `ios_9` boolean: `PhoneCallSource_ANCS` if true, otherwise `PhoneCallSource_ANCS_Legacy`.
    - Calls `event_put` to send the constructed `PebbleEvent`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function sends a `PebbleEvent` to the event system.


---
### prv\_strip\_formatting\_chars<!-- {{#callable:prv_strip_formatting_chars}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/ancs/ancs_phone_call.c#L45>)

Removes formatting characters from a UTF-8 encoded string.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``text``: A pointer to a null-terminated string from which formatting characters will be removed.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calculate the length of the input string `text`.
    - Initialize two pointers, `w_cursor` and `r_cursor`, to the start of the string `text`.
    - Enter a loop that continues until a null character is encountered in the string.
    - Use `utf8_peek_codepoint` to get the next UTF-8 codepoint from `r_cursor` and update `next` to point to the next character.
    - If the codepoint is zero, break the loop as it indicates the end of the string.
    - Check if the codepoint is not a formatting indicator using `codepoint_is_formatting_indicator`.
    - If the codepoint is not a formatting indicator, copy the character from `r_cursor` to `w_cursor` using `utf8_copy_character` and update `w_cursor` by the length of the copied character.
    - Move `r_cursor` to the next character in the string using `next`.
    - After the loop, set the character at `w_cursor` to null to terminate the string.
- **Output**: A modified version of the input string `text` with all formatting characters removed.


---
### ancs\_phone\_call\_handle\_incoming<!-- {{#callable:ancs_phone_call_handle_incoming}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/ancs/ancs_phone_call.c#L68>)

Handles an incoming phone call by processing caller information and generating a call event.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``uid``: A unique identifier for the phone call.
    - ``properties``: Properties of the phone call, which may include flags such as whether the call is from iOS 9.
    - ``notif_attributes``: An array of `ANCSAttribute` pointers containing notification attributes, including caller information.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the caller's name or phone number from `notif_attributes` using the index `FetchedNotifAttributeIndexTitle`.
    - Convert the caller's name or phone number from a `pstring16` format to a standard C string using `pstring_pstring16_to_string`.
    - Remove any formatting characters from the caller ID string using [`prv_strip_formatting_chars`](<#prv_strip_formatting_chars>).
    - Create a `PebblePhoneCaller` object with the cleaned caller ID string using `phone_call_util_create_caller`.
    - Determine if the call is from iOS 9 by checking the `ANCSProperty_iOS9` flag in `properties`.
    - Generate and put a phone call event of type `PhoneEventType_Incoming` using [`prv_put_call_event`](<#prv_put_call_event>), passing the call identifier, caller object, and iOS version flag.
- **Output**: No return value; the function generates a phone call event.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_strip_formatting_chars`](<#prv_strip_formatting_chars>)
    - [`prv_put_call_event`](<#prv_put_call_event>)


---
### ancs\_phone\_call\_handle\_removed<!-- {{#callable:ancs_phone_call_handle_removed}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/ancs/ancs_phone_call.c#L84>)

Handles the removal of a phone call event by triggering a hide event.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``uid``: The unique identifier for the phone call event.
    - ``ios_9``: A boolean indicating if the iOS version is 9 or later.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`prv_put_call_event`](<#prv_put_call_event>) function with `PhoneEventType_Hide` to indicate the call event should be hidden.
    - Passes the `uid` and `ios_9` parameters to [`prv_put_call_event`](<#prv_put_call_event>), with `NULL` for the `caller` parameter.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_put_call_event`](<#prv_put_call_event>)


---
### ancs\_phone\_call\_should\_ignore\_missed\_calls<!-- {{#callable:ancs_phone_call_should_ignore_missed_calls}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/ancs/ancs_phone_call.c#L88>)

Determines if missed calls should be ignored based on the status of a scheduled timer.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `regular_timer_is_scheduled` with `s_missed_call_timer_id` to check if the timer is active.
    - Returns the result of the `regular_timer_is_scheduled` function call.
- **Output**: Returns a boolean value indicating whether the missed call timer is currently scheduled.


---
### prv\_handle\_missed\_call\_timer\_timeout<!-- {{#callable:prv_handle_missed_call_timer_timeout}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/ancs/ancs_phone_call.c#L92>)

Removes the callback for the missed call timer if it is currently scheduled.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``not_used``: A placeholder parameter that is not used in the function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if the missed call timer, identified by `s_missed_call_timer_id`, is currently scheduled using `regular_timer_is_scheduled`.
    - If the timer is scheduled, it removes the callback associated with `s_missed_call_timer_id` using `regular_timer_remove_callback`.
- **Output**: No output is returned as the function has a `void` return type.


---
### ancs\_phone\_call\_temporarily\_block\_missed\_calls<!-- {{#callable:ancs_phone_call_temporarily_block_missed_calls}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/notifications/ancs/ancs_phone_call.c#L98>)

Temporarily blocks missed call notifications for a specified duration.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Defines a constant `BLOCK_MISS_CALL_TIME_S` set to 7 seconds.
    - Checks if a regular timer is already scheduled using `regular_timer_is_scheduled` with `s_missed_call_timer_id`.
    - If a timer is scheduled, it removes the existing timer callback using `regular_timer_remove_callback`.
    - Initializes `s_missed_call_timer_id` with a callback function `prv_handle_missed_call_timer_timeout`.
    - Adds a new timer callback with `regular_timer_add_multisecond_callback` for 7 seconds using `s_missed_call_timer_id`.
- **Output**: No output is returned as the function is of type `void`.
